stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 30 website users. The sample mean is 99.8 ms with a sample standard deviation of 26.5 ms. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 99.8 ms. 95% CI = [89.9, 109.7]. Point estimate: 99.8 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
